headline: USA RARE EARTH DROPS $2.8B: Serra Verde Is the Only Ex-China Source of All 4 Magnetic Rare Earths
snippet: USA Rare Earth (USAR) agreed to acquire Brazil's Serra Verde — the only rare earth producer outside Asia capable of supplying all four magnetic rare earth elements (Nd, Pr, Dy, Tb) at scale — for approximately $2.8 billion in cash and shares, with a U.S. government-backed 15-year offtake already in place. USAR stock surged 13% on the announcement, bringing its 2026 gain to nearly 93%.

headline: AGNICO EAGLE BUYS A WHOLE DISTRICT: Three Finland Deals Worth $3.8B Lock Up 2,492 km² for 500Koz Gold Hub
snippet: Agnico Eagle simultaneously signed definitive agreements to acquire Rupert Resources (~C$2.87B), Aurion Resources ($481M), and B2Gold's Fingold joint-venture stake ($444M), consolidating nearly 2,500 km² in Finland's Central Lapland Greenstone Belt. The combined Ikkari–Kittilä platform targets 500,000 oz/year regional production with over $500M in identified synergies.

headline: SILVER LAPS GOLD: Fourth Straight Weekly Win, 30% Above March Low, as Gold-Silver Ratio Collapses from 104:1 to 64:1
snippet: Silver is dramatically outpacing gold in 2026, posting a fourth consecutive weekly gain and sitting roughly 30% above its March lows as the gold-silver ratio compresses from a peak above 104:1 to around 64:1. Booming industrial demand from solar panels and EVs — combined with investment inflows — is driving a 150%+ gain over the past year.

headline: CLSA LIFTS GOLD TO $5,500 by 2028 as Miners Trade at Rare P/NAV Discount; Ghana's 12% Royalty Bites
snippet: CLSA upgraded its gold price forecasts to $4,840 for 2026, $5,130 for 2027, and $5,500 for 2028 as Strait of Hormuz ceasefire tensions ease. Bank of America notes gold producers trade at just 1.5× P/NAV — a 3% discount to historical averages on reported prices, widening to a 44% discount on spot — while Ghana's new sliding-scale royalty, reaching 12% above $4,500/oz, is beginning to squeeze African producer margins.

headline: WHY CENTRAL BANKS ARE RACING TO BRING THEIR GOLD HOME: Repatriation Surge Signals Deepening Dollar Distrust
snippet: A growing cohort of central banks — particularly in Europe and the Global South — are accelerating repatriation of gold reserves held at the Bank of England and Federal Reserve, signalling deepening distrust of the U.S. dollar system and reinforcing the structural bid under gold prices. Central banks collectively bought 2,575 tonnes over the past five years, and repatriation adds a further urgency to domestic custody.

headline: U.S. GOLDMINING BOOTS UP 2026 SEASON at Alaska's Whistler Gold-Copper Project — District-Scale Targets in Sight
snippet: U.S. GoldMining Inc. completed pre-mobilization at its Whistler Gold-Copper Project in south-central Alaska, positioning its exploration team to commence diamond core drilling on high-priority near-deposit targets within the Whistler-Raintree corridor as soon as ground conditions allow. The project sits in a district-scale porphyry system that the company believes has meaningful resource upside.

headline: KITCO: After the $5,600 Record, Does the Gold-Silver Rally Have Legs — Or Is a Technical Pullback Overdue?
snippet: With gold consolidating in the $4,800 range after touching $5,600 earlier in Q1 and silver posting a multi-week winning streak, Kitco analysts weigh whether structural tailwinds — central bank demand, de-dollarization, and easing rate expectations — are sufficient to resume the advance, or whether a technical correction is building before the next leg higher.
